title: Mastodon
description: Free, open-source decentralized social network server based on ActivityPub - self-hosted microblogging for the Fediverse.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Mastodon web (Puma/Rails) container from ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon, HTTP on port 3000. Mastodon is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack: this single template covers ONLY the web server, so you MUST deploy these companion services separately for a working instance: 1) SIDEKIQ WORKER - same image ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon:v4.4.18 with command ['bundle','exec','sidekiq'] and the SAME env vars as this web container; this runs ALL background queues (federation push/pull, mailers, media processing, scheduled tasks) and is MANDATORY - without it nothing federates. 2) STREAMING SERVER - image ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon-streaming:v4.4.18 listening on port 4000 (the real-time WebSocket/timeline API); route /api/v1/streaming to it. Both companions share the same Postgres + Redis add-ons created here. REQUIRED BEFORE FIRST BOOT (rotate every placeholder): generate the cryptographic secrets - run 'docker run --rm ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon:v4.4.18 bundle exec rake secret' to produce SECRET_KEY_BASE and OTP_SECRET; run 'bundle exec rake mastodon:webpush:generate_vapid_key' for VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY/VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY; and run 'bundle exec rails db:encryption:init' for the three ACTIVE_RECORD_ENCRYPTION_* keys. Set LOCAL_DOMAIN to your real public host BEFORE federating - it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed after going live. FIRST-RUN SETUP: exec into the web pod and run 'RAILS_ENV=production bundle exec rake db:setup' (creates schema + seeds), then create an owner account with 'bin/tootctl accounts create <user> --email <email> --confirmed --role Owner'. EMAIL: set SMTP_* env vars (not preset here) so confirmation/notification mail works. OPTIONAL EXTERNALS (omitted from addons, wire up later): full-text search needs Elasticsearch/OpenSearch (set ES_ENABLED=true + ES_HOST/ES_PORT and run 'tootctl search deploy'); media at scale should use an S3-compatible bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) via S3_ENABLED=true + S3_BUCKET/AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY instead of the local volume.
